Board labels this dimension_pattern=q1 / slope_class=independent_one_slope (not a true labelled q2 covariance block). Recovery-only crossed n_lvl{10,20,30} x 30-seed ladder bundling beta with gamma/student: 90/90 beta fits converged with pdHess=TRUE. No interval, coverage, labelled, or multi-slope claim.

Representative row for ALL beyond-admitted animal mu shapes: labelled/correlated blocks at any true q, and unlabelled terms with 2+ slopes (e.g. animal(1 + x1 + x2 | id)). Every such shape hits the same cli_abort ("only unlabelled q=1 intercepts" / "only ... intercept-only or one-slope") regardless of its real covariance size, so q_gate=q2 here is a placeholder, not a validated q2 block.

Intercept-only (q1) unlabelled animal() term in sigma only. Board fit_status=point_fit, interval_status=unsupported, coverage_status=planned. mu and sigma animal effects cannot be requested simultaneously (R/drmTMB.R:4408-4415 aborts "beta structured animal effects currently support one endpoint at a time" -- q2/q4 beta location-scale blocks are explicitly post-v1.0 design work).

Representative row for one-slope, labelled, or larger unlabelled animal sigma terms. Sigma is stricter than mu: ONLY intercept-only is admitted, no one-slope allowance at all (unlike mu). Confirmed directly by test: sigma ~ animal(1 + x | id, pedigree = ped) -> "intercept-only structured terms".

S5 promotion ratified (D-43 round 2 passed; maintainer PR review 2026-07-19), scored under the frozen 2026-07-17 estimand/interval/coverage contract on the unchanged machine-strict conditional-Beta interior DGP. Leading with the worst-in-arm result per the frozen S0 section 5.3 rule: shared_g1024_m04 profile alpha_intercept (fixef:sd_phylo(spp_id):(Intercept), the log-baseline latent-SD intercept of sd(spp_id, level = "phylogenetic") ~ x_tau, truth log(0.30)) covers 1119/1199=0.9333 (MCSE 0.0072; exact 95% CI 0.9176-0.9467) -- mildly anti-conservative, not nominal -- before the nominal results below. Both exact promotion arms (distinct_g1024_m04, shared_g1024_m04; N=1200 attempted replicates each; g=1024,m=4; profile method, 95% two-sided) pass the frozen S0 section 5 calibration gate for both fixed direct-SD regression coefficients: distinct_g1024_m04 alpha_intercept 1130/1200=0.9417 (CI 0.9269-0.9542) and alpha_x (the latent-SD slope, truth 0.25) 1132/1200=0.9433 (CI 0.9287-0.9557), profile_finite_rate=1.000, zero failures; shared_g1024_m04 alpha_intercept 1119/1199=0.9333 (CI 0.9176-0.9467) and alpha_x 1140/1199=0.9508 (CI 0.9370-0.9623), profile_finite_rate=0.99917 (1/1200 tmbprofile calls failed on both coefficients: replicate 407, seed 1989879593, extra_coverage seed source, fit_success=TRUE, pdHess=TRUE; per the pre-registered S0 section 5.1 exclusion policy, coverage is computed over the remaining 1199 finite profiles and the exclusion is disclosed, not silently dropped). Both Wald and profile intervals are computed side by side and neither is assumed superior a priori (S0 section 4); Wald coverage is observed to be closely similar to profile throughout (distinct wald: alpha_intercept 0.9425, alpha_x 0.9450; shared wald: alpha_intercept 0.9342, alpha_x 0.9525) -- an observation, not a profile-superiority claim. Per the S0 section 7 seed policy, replicates 1-400 of each 1200-replicate arm reuse the frozen point-fit-recovery seeds, so this coverage evidence and the prior point-fit-recovery evidence are correlated draws from the same DGP realisations, not two independent confirmations. The residual mild anti-conservatism, most visible on the shared arm, should be read against a low phylogenetic effective sample size at this design (mean_effective_n_proxy ~2.3 despite g=1024, driven by mean off-diagonal phylogenetic correlation ~0.46), not attributed to raw g. Tau is latent location SD, not family sigma or phi. This promotes inference_ready_with_caveats for exactly the two g=1024,m=4 promotion arms; the shared g=256,m=2 stress cell and the g in {256,512} context cells give the directional coverage-vs-g trend only and do not promote (S0 section 8), and the shared_g256_m02 HOLD (one warning, pdHess=0.9975) is reported descriptively only, never pooled into this or any promotion or gate, and is not repaired. Excluded (S0 section 9 fence, verbatim): q>1; family-sigma phylogeny; random/hierarchical RHS in sd(); labels/slopes; REML; missing routes; other families; a coverage-correcting estimator (this arc assesses calibration, it does not build a correction); neighbouring SDs/species counts; supported/nominal claims; the shared_g256_m02 HOLD; the stopped campaign 1c9bfd5f; PR #788.

Labelled covariance, q2/q4, intercept-plus-slope and slope-only phylogeny, multiple phylogenetic terms, simultaneous ordinary mu random effects, phylogeny in family sigma, direct sd() regression outside mc-0017's exact unlabelled q1 case, REML, zero_one_beta(), and missing/external-data combinations remain rejected or deferred.
